project.title: Puf6 ushers correct rRNA compaction during ribosome assembly
project.projectDescription: Chemical cross-linking coupled to mass spectrometry was used to study the complex between the nucleolar factor, Puf6, and the yeast 60S ribosome. Disuccinimidyl suberate (DSS) was used as the cross-linking reagent.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Chemical cross-linking was performed following previously described procedures (Leitner et al., Nat. Protoc., 2014). The sample was digested using endoproteinase Lys-C followed by trypsin and the digest was fractionated by size-exclusion chromatography. Two SEC fractions were analyzed in duplicate by data-dependent LC-MS/MS on a Thermo Orbitrap Elite system. File numbers 156/157 and 158/159, respectively, are replicate injections of the same fraction. Additional details are provided in the accompanying publication.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: MS/MS spectra were searched using xQuest/xProphet (Walzthoeni et al., Nat. Methods, 2012), version 2.1.4, against the sequences of target proteins (Puf6 and ribosomal proteins) and identified contaminants.  Additional search settings were as follows: Enzyme = trypsin, maximum number of missed cleavages = 2, MS mass tolerances -5 to +5 ppm, MS/MS mass tolerance = 0.2 Da for “common”-type fragment ions and 0.3 Da for “xlink”-type fragment ions. The original scoring scheme of xQuest (Rinner et al., Nat. Methods, 2008) was used. xProphet was used to adjust the false discovery rate to <5% at the nonredundant peptide pair level.
keywords: Chemical cross-linking; dss; structural proteomics; ribosome; ribosomal assembly

references.referenceLine: Gerhardy S, Oborská-Oplová M, Gillet L, Börner R, van Nues R, Leitner A, Michel E, Petkowski JJ, Granneman S, Sigel RKO, Aebersold R, Panse VG. Puf6 primes 60S pre-ribosome nuclear export at low temperature. Nat Commun. 2021 12(1):4696

Characteristics[ReductionReagent]: reduced with 12 mM DTT at 37 °C for 30 min
Characteristics[AlkylationReagent]: alkylated with 40 mM iodoacetamide at room temperature in the dark for 30 min
Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic: Three series of wildtype yeast cells (BY4741) were grown in liquid YPD media at 20 °C, 25 °C, 30 °C, and 37 °C and harvested at OD600 ~ 3.
Comment[Instrument]: Eksigent NanoLC Ultra 1D Plus system (Eksigent, Dublin, CA)
Comment[Separation]: 75-μm-diameter, 40 cm-long newObjective emitters packed with Magic C18 AQ 3 μm resin (Michrom BioResources)
Comment[GradientTime]: linear gradient of 2-to-30% Buffer A for 120 min
Comment[CollisionEnergy]: collision energy was set to 0.0625 × m/z—10.5 with a 15-eV collision energy spread regardless of the precursor charge state.
